2015 Honda Civic SE Sedan, 130,772 miles, all black inside and out. The SE was Honda's Special Edition, slotted right between the base LX and the higher EX, and it is the trim Civic shoppers hunt for. It bundles roughly $1,100 in added equipment over the LX, which is why clean SE models hold value and move fast. Easy on gas, easy to drive, a perfect first car or daily commuter for the North Shore. The AC blows ice cold, so your summer commute stays cool and comfortable.The standout is Honda LaneWatch. Flip your right turn signal and a camera in the passenger mirror shows your entire blind spot on the 7 inch touchscreen, so lane changes feel a lot safer. That same screen runs Display Audio with Bluetooth and smartphone integration, plus a backup camera. The SE also adds automatic climate control, cruise control, push button start, smart entry, USB input, and alloy wheels. Under the hood is Honda's proven 1.8 liter four cylinder paired with a smooth automatic CVT and front wheel drive, EPA rated 30 city and 39 highway. These engines routinely run past 300,000 miles on basic maintenance, so at 130,772 miles this one is barely broken in, and the 2015 Civic earned an IIHS Top Safety Pick.The Maxim Autos DifferenceIndependent inspection report included with every vehicle15 day / 500 mile Illinois powertrain protection, extended coverage availableMetal plates issued on the spot, drive home todayTransparent pricing, no hidden feesFinancing for all credit levelsSkokie, IL. 2019 Hyundai Tucson SE 4WD, 70,524 miles, champagne metallic on beautiful light beige leather interior. Good on gas for an all wheel drive SUV, with up to 25 mpg on the highway. The AC blows ice cold to keep you comfortable through the hottest Chicago summer days. The Tucson SE brings premium safety and tech into an affordable, easy to own compact SUV, which is why it punches above its price. The right size for the city, comfortable and quiet on the highway, and simple to park, it makes a smart daily driver for the North Shore. What sets the SE apart is how much comes standard. Hyundai's safety suite includes Forward Collision Avoidance Assist with automatic emergency braking, Lane Keeping Assist, and Driver Attention Warning, so the tech is watching the road with you. The 7 inch touchscreen runs Apple CarPlay and Android Auto with Bluetooth, and you also get 17 inch alloy wheels, automatic headlights, a backup camera, and a 60/40 split folding rear seat for flexible cargo space. Power comes from a 2.0 liter four cylinder with 161 horsepower and a smooth 6 speed automatic, EPA rated 25 mpg. The ride soaks up rough roads, the cabin stays quiet, and Hyundai's strong reliability reputation means it just keeps going.
